title: Define predicate types and routing module root
purpose: Establishes the predicate type system that forms the declarative tier of the two-tier routing model (ADR-003). These types are the building blocks for content-aware subscriptions: comparison operators, boolean combinators, range checks, and field-existence tests. The evaluation engine (ROUTING-002) and predicate compiler (ROUTING-003) consume these types; this brief defines them without implementing evaluation logic.
task: Create the routing module root and the predicate type definitions. Define the Predicate enum with variants for comparison, boolean combination, range, and field-existence. Define supporting types: ComparisonOp (six relational operators), FieldValue (typed operand values), and FieldPath (dot-notation message field accessor). Establish the missing-field contract: predicates against absent fields evaluate to false without error. This brief defines types only — evaluation logic belongs to ROUTING-002, compilation to ROUTING-003.

requirements.title: Define comparison operator enum and comparison predicate variant
requirements.spec: The predicate module SHALL define a ComparisonOp enum with exactly six variants (Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, Lte) and a Predicate::Comparison variant taking a FieldPath, ComparisonOp, and FieldValue. The ComparisonOp enum SHALL NOT include pattern-matching or regex operators.
acceptance: ComparisonOp enum has exactly six variants: Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, Lte.
Predicate::Comparison { field: FieldPath, op: ComparisonOp, value: FieldValue } constructs a valid predicate.
ComparisonOp implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.
All six ComparisonOp variants round-trip through Debug formatting.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:1-90 — new file to create; define `ComparisonOp`, supporting `FieldPath`/`FieldValue`, `Predicate::Comparison`, and local construction/debug tests.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-001.json:14-30 — exact R1 spec/acceptance: six comparison operators only, no pattern/regex operators.
docs/design/routing/design.json:6-14 — ADR-003 context: predicates are declarative, analyzable, and compile to functions later; this brief must stay type-only.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:4-26 — existing liminal style for public enums/structs: doc comments plus `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, `Eq` where possible.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lints deny unsafe, clippy all/pedantic, `unwrap`, `expect`, and `panic`; tests and helpers should avoid lint escapes.
context: `crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs` does not exist yet; `crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs` is only a placeholder comment, so no existing predicate API must be preserved.

`ComparisonOp` should be an enum with exactly `Eq`, `Ne`, `Gt`, `Lt`, `Gte`, `Lte`. Deriving `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq` matches nearby style; `Copy` is extra but harmless, while adding `Regex`, `Like`, `Contains`, etc. would violate the brief.

`Predicate::Comparison` should be a named-field variant: `Comparison { field: FieldPath, op: ComparisonOp, value: FieldValue }`. Derive `Debug` on `Predicate` too because CN4 applies to all public types.

Keep this module as data definitions plus construction tests only. Do not add `evaluate`, field access traits, routing-table types, or predicate compiler hooks; those are explicitly reserved for ROUTING-002/003.
scout.approach: Implement R1 together with the supporting R4 types in the new `predicate.rs`: define `ComparisonOp` first, then `FieldValue`, `FieldPath`, and `Predicate`. Add a small `#[cfg(test)]` module that constructs a comparison predicate and checks all six `Debug` strings with `format!("{variant:?}")`; avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` even in tests because workspace clippy denies them globally.
scout.notes: Because `FieldValue` includes `Float(f64)`, do not try to derive `Eq` for `FieldValue` or `Predicate`; keep `Eq` limited to `ComparisonOp` and `FieldPath`.

files_changed.note: Added ComparisonOp with exactly Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, Lte plus Predicate::Comparison using FieldPath, ComparisonOp, and FieldValue; included construction and Debug round-trip tests.

dev.how: Defined the six-variant `ComparisonOp` enum with `Clone`, `Copy`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, and `Eq`, intentionally omitting pattern/regex operators. Added the named-field `Predicate::Comparison { field, op, value }` shape and tests proving valid construction and all six Debug strings.

checklist.note: Comparison operators are defined in `routing::predicate` and usable in `Predicate::Comparison`.

stories.note: The type layer can now express comparisons such as amount greater than a value and region equal to text for predicate subscription declarations.

acceptance.criterion: ComparisonOp enum has exactly six variants: Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, Lte.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:3-16 defines ComparisonOp with only Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, and Lte.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate::Comparison { field: FieldPath, op: ComparisonOp, value: FieldValue } constructs a valid predicate.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:56-65 defines the named fields; test routing::predicate::tests::comparison_predicate_constructs at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:92-108 constructs and compares one successfully.

acceptance.criterion: ComparisonOp implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:2 derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq for ComparisonOp.

acceptance.criterion: All six ComparisonOp variants round-trip through Debug formatting.
acceptance.evidence: test routing::predicate::tests::comparison_debug_round_trips_all_variants at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:110-118 checks Debug output for Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, and Lte; cargo test -p liminal passed.

requirements.title: Define boolean combinator predicates for arbitrary composition
requirements.spec: The Predicate enum SHALL include And, Or, and Not variants for composing predicates into arbitrarily nested expressions. And and Or SHALL accept a Vec<Predicate>. Not SHALL accept a Box<Predicate>. Composition depth SHALL NOT be artificially limited.
acceptance: Predicate::And(vec![p1, p2]) composes two predicates with logical AND.
Predicate::Or(vec![p1, p2]) composes two predicates with logical OR.
Predicate::Not(Box::new(p)) negates a single predicate.
Nested composition Predicate::And(vec![Predicate::Comparison{..}, Predicate::Or(vec![..])]) constructs without error.
Empty And(vec![]) and Or(vec![]) are valid constructions.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:40-120 — add `Predicate::And(Vec<Predicate>)`, `Predicate::Or(Vec<Predicate>)`, and `Predicate::Not(Box<Predicate>)` in the same enum.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-001.json:31-49 — R2 acceptance: tuple variants, nested composition, and empty `And`/`Or` are valid constructions.
docs/design/routing/checklist.json:5-12 — C2 is part of the predicate-type checklist and should be satisfied without implementing evaluation.
docs/design/routing/stories.json:5-13 — S2 user-facing goal: express complex routing declaratively without writing a routing function.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-002.json:34-44 — later evaluation semantics for short-circuiting and empty `And`/`Or`; useful context but out of scope here.
context: The recursive enum needs indirection only for `Not`; the brief specifically wants `And(Vec<Predicate>)`, `Or(Vec<Predicate>)`, and `Not(Box<Predicate>)`.
Composition depth must not be artificially limited. Do not add a max-depth constant, constructor validation, or a fallible builder.

Empty `And(vec![])` and `Or(vec![])` must construct successfully now. ROUTING-002 later defines that empty `And` evaluates true and empty `Or` evaluates false; this brief should not encode that behavior.
Derive `Clone`, `Debug`, and `PartialEq` on `Predicate` so nested predicates can be cloned, debug-printed, and asserted in tests.
scout.approach: Extend the new `Predicate` enum with the three tuple variants exactly as the acceptance examples show. Add type-construction tests that build two comparison predicates, combine them with `And` and `Or`, wrap one with `Not`, and build the nested `And([Comparison, Or([...])])` shape; separately assert empty vectors construct by matching on `Predicate::And(children)`/`Predicate::Or(children)` and checking `children.is_empty()`.
scout.notes: Do not add smart constructors that reject empty boolean lists; the acceptance explicitly requires empty construction to be valid.

files_changed.note: Added Predicate::And(Vec<Self>), Predicate::Or(Vec<Self>), and Predicate::Not(Box<Self>) plus nested and empty composition tests.

dev.how: Extended `Predicate` with recursive boolean combinators matching the required public construction forms while adding no depth limit, constructors, validation, or evaluation behavior. Tests construct AND, OR, NOT, nested AND/OR, and empty AND/OR values.
dev.deviation: Used `Vec<Self>` and `Box<Self>` inside the enum definition rather than spelling `Vec<Predicate>` and `Box<Predicate>` to satisfy clippy's `use_self` lint; the public variant constructors remain `Predicate::And(Vec<Predicate>)`, `Predicate::Or(Vec<Predicate>)`, and `Predicate::Not(Box<Predicate>)` in use.

checklist.note: Boolean combinators for arbitrary nested predicate composition are defined.

stories.note: Application developers can represent complex declarative routing rules with AND, OR, and NOT without a routing function.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate::And(vec![p1, p2]) composes two predicates with logical AND.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:66-68 defines And(Vec<Self>); test routing::predicate::tests::boolean_combinators_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:133 and 138 constructs/asserts a two-child And.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate::Or(vec![p1, p2]) composes two predicates with logical OR.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:68-69 defines Or(Vec<Self>); test routing::predicate::tests::boolean_combinators_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:134 and 139 constructs/asserts a two-child Or.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate::Not(Box::new(p)) negates a single predicate.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:70-71 defines Not(Box<Self>); test routing::predicate::tests::boolean_combinators_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:135 and 140 constructs/asserts Not(Box::new(...)).

acceptance.criterion: Nested composition Predicate::And(vec![Predicate::Comparison{..}, Predicate::Or(vec![..])]) constructs without error.
acceptance.evidence: test routing::predicate::tests::boolean_combinators_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:136-144 constructs and asserts nested And containing Or.

acceptance.criterion: Empty And(vec![]) and Or(vec![]) are valid constructions.
acceptance.evidence: test routing::predicate::tests::empty_boolean_combinators_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:147-151 constructs/asserts empty And and Or; cargo test -p liminal passed.

requirements.title: Define range-check and field-existence predicate variants
requirements.spec: The Predicate enum SHALL include a Range variant taking a FieldPath and two FieldValue bounds (lower inclusive, upper inclusive) for numeric and string field comparisons. The Predicate enum SHALL include an Exists variant taking a FieldPath for testing field presence. Range SHALL NOT support exclusive bounds in this brief.
acceptance: Predicate::Range { field: FieldPath, lower: FieldValue, upper: FieldValue } constructs a range predicate.
Predicate::Exists { field: FieldPath } constructs a field-existence predicate.
Range with FieldValue::Integer(100) lower and FieldValue::Integer(200) upper compiles without error.
Range with FieldValue::Text("a".into()) lower and FieldValue::Text("z".into()) upper compiles without error.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:40-130 — add `Predicate::Range { field, lower, upper }` and `Predicate::Exists { field }` variants.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-001.json:50-67 — exact R3 acceptance for inclusive range shape and field-existence shape.
docs/design/routing/checklist.json:7-11 — C3/C4/C5 show range, existence, and missing-field contract are related but not all implemented as runtime behavior here.
docs/design/routing/design.json:17-24 — goals mention predicate comparisons/range checks as part of later routing behavior.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-002.json:54-63 — future inclusive range evaluation and missing-field false semantics; do not implement in ROUTING-001.
context: `Range` should use named fields and inclusive bounds only: `Range { field: FieldPath, lower: FieldValue, upper: FieldValue }`.
`Exists` should only carry `FieldPath`; do not define a separate field-accessor trait or message representation in this brief.
The type layer should permit both integer and text bounds as shown by acceptance. It should not validate bound ordering or type compatibility during construction.
No exclusive-bound type, flags, or `std::ops::RangeBounds` wrapper should be introduced; the spec explicitly says exclusive bounds are not supported in this brief.
scout.approach: Add the two variants to `Predicate` and cover them with construction tests: one integer range (`100..=200` represented by two `FieldValue::Integer` bounds), one text range (`FieldValue::Text("a".into())` to `FieldValue::Text("z".into())`), and one `Exists { field: FieldPath::new("region") }`. Keep all behavior as plain data construction; comparisons happen in ROUTING-002.
scout.notes: A tempting overbuild is to introduce a `RangeBound` enum or validation constructor; avoid it because it adds API not requested and can conflict with the inclusive-only contract.

files_changed.note: Added inclusive Predicate::Range and Predicate::Exists variants with integer/text range and existence construction tests.

dev.how: Added `Predicate::Range { field, lower, upper }` with only inclusive bounds represented by two `FieldValue`s, and `Predicate::Exists { field }` with no validation or evaluation logic. Tests cover integer ranges, text ranges, and field-existence construction.

checklist.note: Range predicates for numeric and string field bounds are represented by `Predicate::Range`.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate::Range { field: FieldPath, lower: FieldValue, upper: FieldValue } constructs a range predicate.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:72-80 defines Range with field/lower/upper; test routing::predicate::tests::range_and_exists_predicates_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:155-176 constructs/asserts an integer range.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate::Exists { field: FieldPath } constructs a field-existence predicate.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:81-85 defines Exists with field; test routing::predicate::tests::range_and_exists_predicates_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:165-190 constructs/asserts Exists.

acceptance.criterion: Range with FieldValue::Integer(100) lower and FieldValue::Integer(200) upper compiles without error.
acceptance.evidence: test routing::predicate::tests::range_and_exists_predicates_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:155-159 uses FieldValue::Integer(100) and FieldValue::Integer(200); cargo check -p liminal and cargo test -p liminal passed.

acceptance.criterion: Range with FieldValue::Text("a".into()) lower and FieldValue::Text("z".into()) upper compiles without error.
acceptance.evidence: test routing::predicate::tests::range_and_exists_predicates_construct at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:160-164 uses FieldValue::Text("a") and FieldValue::Text("z"); cargo check -p liminal and cargo test -p liminal passed.

requirements.title: Define FieldValue enum, FieldPath accessor, and missing-field contract
requirements.spec: The predicate module SHALL define a FieldValue enum with variants for String, Integer (i64), Float (f64), Boolean, and Null. The module SHALL define a FieldPath type wrapping a dot-notation string with a segments accessor. Predicates evaluated against a message missing the referenced field SHALL evaluate to false without returning an error. The FieldValue type SHALL NOT carry binary or structured (nested object) values.
acceptance: FieldValue has exactly five variants: Text(String), Integer(i64), Float(f64), Boolean(bool), Null.
FieldPath::new("user.address.city") constructs a valid path.
FieldPath::segments() on "user.address.city" returns an iterator yielding ["user", "address", "city"].
FieldPath::new("name") with no dots yields a single segment ["name"].
FieldValue implements Clone, Debug, and PartialEq.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:1-70 — define `FieldValue` and `FieldPath` before `Predicate` so variants can use them.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-001.json:68-86 — exact R4 acceptance: `Text(String)`, `Integer(i64)`, `Float(f64)`, `Boolean(bool)`, `Null`, plus `FieldPath::new` and `segments()` behavior.
docs/design/routing/design.json:49-58 — constraints CN4/CN5: public types need `Debug`; future evaluation must avoid per-message allocation.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-34 — local constructor style: public data type with a small `new` method and `#[must_use]` on constructors.
Cargo.toml:17-23 — clippy pedantic and no-panic lints make `#[must_use]` and no `unwrap`/`expect` important even for simple helpers.
context: The acceptance says the string variant is `Text(String)`, not `String`; use exactly `Text`, `Integer`, `Float`, `Boolean`, and `Null` and do not add binary/object/list/map variants.
`FieldPath` should be an owned wrapper, e.g. `pub struct FieldPath(String);`, with `FieldPath::new(path: impl Into<String>) -> Self` so both `&str` and `String` construct cleanly.
`segments()` should return a borrowing iterator over `&str`, typically `self.0.split('.')`; this avoids allocating a `Vec` and preserves CN5 for the later evaluator.
The missing-field contract belongs in documentation on `Predicate` or `FieldPath` for now. Do not implement runtime missing-field handling until ROUTING-002.
scout.approach: Define `FieldValue` with `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]` and `FieldPath` with `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]`. Implement `#[must_use] pub fn new(path: impl Into<String>) -> Self` and `#[must_use] pub fn segments(&self) -> std::str::Split<'_, char>` (or an equivalent `impl Iterator<Item = &str>`), then test `FieldPath::new("user.address.city").segments().collect::<Vec<_>>() == ["user", "address", "city"]` and the single-segment `"name"` case.
scout.notes: The brief does not specify validation for empty paths, leading/trailing dots, or repeated dots. Do not invent an error type or fallible constructor; that would ripple into every predicate variant and exceed this type-definition brief.

files_changed.note: Added FieldValue, FieldPath, FieldPath::new, borrowing segments iterator, and missing-field contract documentation.

dev.how: Defined `FieldValue` with exactly `Text(String)`, `Integer(i64)`, `Float(f64)`, `Boolean(bool)`, and `Null`, deriving `Clone`, `Debug`, and `PartialEq`. Defined owned `FieldPath` with a `#[must_use]` constructor and a non-allocating borrowed `segments()` iterator; documented that missing referenced fields must evaluate false once evaluation is implemented later.
dev.deviation: Did not put `#[must_use]` on `segments()` because clippy flags it as redundant for the returned iterator type; the method remains a borrowed, allocation-free segment accessor as scouted.

checklist.note: The missing-field false-without-error contract is documented on `Predicate`; runtime evaluation remains out of scope for ROUTING-002.

stories.note: The predicate value/path types support declarative examples such as amount comparisons and region text checks.

acceptance.criterion: FieldValue has exactly five variants: Text(String), Integer(i64), Float(f64), Boolean(bool), Null.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:18-31 defines FieldValue with only Text(String), Integer(i64), Float(f64), Boolean(bool), and Null.

acceptance.criterion: FieldPath::new("user.address.city") constructs a valid path.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:33-42 defines FieldPath and FieldPath::new; test routing::predicate::tests::field_path_segments_are_borrowed_dot_parts at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:195 constructs FieldPath::new("user.address.city").

acceptance.criterion: FieldPath::segments() on "user.address.city" returns an iterator yielding ["user", "address", "city"].
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:44-47 returns self.0.split('.'); test routing::predicate::tests::field_path_segments_are_borrowed_dot_parts at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:195-201 collects and asserts ["user", "address", "city"].

acceptance.criterion: FieldPath::new("name") with no dots yields a single segment ["name"].
acceptance.evidence: test routing::predicate::tests::field_path_segments_are_borrowed_dot_parts at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:197-202 constructs FieldPath::new("name") and asserts ["name"].

acceptance.criterion: FieldValue implements Clone, Debug, and PartialEq.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:19 derives Clone, Debug, and PartialEq for FieldValue.

requirements.title: Wire routing module root with pub mod and re-exports
requirements.spec: The routing module root SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports per CN3. All public predicate types SHALL be re-exported from the routing module root.
acceptance: routing/mod.rs contains pub mod predicate and no function or trait definitions.
Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, and FieldPath are accessible via use crate::routing::{Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, FieldPath}.
No logic (functions, trait impls, match arms) exists in mod.rs.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:1-1 — currently only `// routing module`; replace with `pub mod predicate;` and `pub use predicate::{ComparisonOp, FieldPath, FieldValue, Predicate};`.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — existing module-root convention: `pub mod` declarations followed by `pub use` re-exports only.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-8 — crate root currently does not declare `pub mod routing;`; `crate::routing::{...}` acceptance will not compile unless this is added.
docs/design/routing/design.json:32-46 — intended routing module layout and CN3 note that subsystem roots are pub-mod/pub-use only.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-001.json:87-104 — R5 acceptance for root exports and no logic in `routing/mod.rs`.
context: `routing/mod.rs` already exists as a placeholder, even though the brief marks it as create. Treat it as a replace/modify of the placeholder line, not a second file.
CN3 is strict for `mod.rs`: no functions, trait definitions, impl blocks, match arms, constants, or helper logic. A two-line root matching `channel/mod.rs` is enough.

All public predicate types must be reachable as `crate::routing::{Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, FieldPath}`. Inside this crate, that also requires `pub mod routing;` in `crates/liminal/src/lib.rs`, which is missing today.
The brief file list omits `lib.rs`, but the acceptance requires crate-level module visibility; flag this during implementation rather than leaving `routing` uncompiled.
scout.approach: Make `routing/mod.rs` contain only `pub mod predicate;` plus one `pub use predicate::{ComparisonOp, FieldPath, FieldValue, Predicate};` line. Then add `pub mod routing;` to `lib.rs` near the other module declarations; no crate-root re-export is required unless reviewers ask for `liminal::{Predicate, ...}` rather than `liminal::routing::{...}`.
scout.notes: If `lib.rs` is not updated, `predicate.rs` and `routing/mod.rs` may not be compiled by `cargo test -p liminal`, causing a false sense of completion.

files_changed.note: Replaced placeholder comment with only `pub mod predicate;` and a `pub use` of the public predicate types.
Added `pub mod routing;` so `crate::routing::{Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, FieldPath}` is compiled and accessible.
Added a re-export test using `crate::routing::{Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, FieldPath}` aliases.

dev.how: Made `routing/mod.rs` a CN3-only module root containing a public submodule declaration and re-exports for all public predicate types. Added the crate-root routing module declaration required for the acceptance path to compile; no crate-root re-export of predicate types was added.
dev.deviation: Modified `crates/liminal/src/lib.rs` even though it was omitted from the brief's file list, because the scout findings identified it as necessary for the explicit `crate::routing::{...}` acceptance criterion and otherwise the routing module would not compile into the crate.

acceptance.criterion: routing/mod.rs contains pub mod predicate and no function or trait definitions.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:1-3 contains only pub mod predicate and pub use predicate::{...}; no fn or trait definitions are present.

acceptance.criterion: Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, and FieldPath are accessible via use crate::routing::{Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, FieldPath}.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:6 exposes pub mod routing; crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:3 re-exports all four types; test routing::predicate::tests::routing_root_re_exports_predicate_types at crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:204-225 imports and uses them via crate::routing.

acceptance.criterion: No logic (functions, trait impls, match arms) exists in mod.rs.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:1-3 is only a pub mod declaration plus pub use re-export; repository search over affected source found no fn/impl/match entries in routing/mod.rs.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement predicate evaluation logic — evaluation belongs to ROUTING-002.
SHALL NOT implement predicate-to-function compilation — compilation belongs to ROUTING-003.
SHALL NOT define routing table or subscription types — those belong to ROUTING-002.
SHALL NOT define backpressure or capacity types — those belong to ROUTING-005.
SHALL NOT add serialization/deserialization derives unless required for type construction tests.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo test -p liminal passes with all predicate type construction tests green.
routing/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic.
predicate.rs is under 500 lines including tests (CN1).
